data.title: Tech Voices: Trump vs. Intel CEO, Amazon's $1B fed discount
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of remarks, statements and announcements that could impact the technology sector.

  • President Trump called for Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) CEO Lip-Bu Tan to immediately resign over his alleged ties to China.

"The CEO of INTEL is highly CONFLICTED and must resign, immediately. There is no other solution to this problem. Thank you for your attention to this problem!" Trump wrote Thursday on Truth Social.

  • Trump's post comes after U.S. Sen. Tom Cotton questioned Tan's ties to Chinese companies and a past legal case involving a company Tan previously led, Cadence Design (CDNS). 

Cotton reportedly wrote Intel's (NASDAQ:INTC) chairman this week expressing "concern about the security and integrity of Intel’s operations and its potential impact on U.S. national security," according to CNBC.

“Intel is required to be a responsible steward of American taxpayer dollars and to comply with applicable security regulations,” Cotton added. “Mr. Tan’s associations raise questions about Intel’s ability to fulfill these obligations.”

  • Amazon Web Services (NASDAQ:AMZN) will give discounts of up to $1 billion for cloud adoption, modernization, and training services provided to U.S. federal agencies through the end of 2028.

“The Trump Administration is rapidly modernizing out-of-date government systems, streamlining processes, and delivering savings to the American taxpayer through centralized procurement,” said Michael Rigas, acting administrator of the General Services Administration, in a statement. 

"Through this new agreement with AWS, federal agencies will be able to enhance delivery of critical services, leverage cloud and advanced AI technologies, and dramatically reduce costs," Rigas added.

The AWS agreement will provide up to a total of $1 billion in direct incentive credits aggregated across federal civilian agencies, the GSA added.

data.title: Swire Pacific reports 1H results
data.description: • Swire Pacific press release (OTCPK:SWRAY): 1H underlying profit attributable to shareholders of HK$5,476 million.

data.title: Spectrum Brands targets $160M free cash flow in 2025 amid supply chain reset and tariff volatility
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Spectrum Brands Holdings, Inc. (SPB) Q3 2025

Management View

  • David M. Maura, Executive Chairman & CEO, described Q3 as a period of significant operational disruption due to a rapid rise in U.S. tariffs on Chinese imports, stating “operating when the cost of your products can more than double overnight, is something we never really thought we'd experience.” He explained that the company responded by pausing virtually all finished good purchases from China, aggressively negotiating supplier pricing, and prioritizing cost reductions, including workforce reductions and shrinking real estate. Maura indicated, “we now expect to reduce our costs by over $50 million in the fiscal year, fiscal '25.”
  • Maura highlighted a rebound as “regular supply is now back on,” and reported that with the reduction in Chinese tariff rates, the company has resumed strategic purchasing and is seeing “sales levels are already improving.”
  • Maura noted strategic progress in supplier diversification, targeting non-Chinese sourcing alternatives for the majority of Global Pet Care purchases by year-end, and continued expansion of non-Chinese sourcing in Home & Personal Care through fiscal '25 and into '26.
  • The CEO emphasized a disciplined approach to pricing, stating that tariff-related pricing negotiations led to temporary halts in shipments to key customers, but “we now have tariff-related pricing in place with practically all of our customers.”
  • Maura announced continued share repurchases, “We, in fact, bought back 900,000 shares… year-to-date through today, we have repurchased approximately 4 million shares for roughly $300 million.”
  • Jeremy W. Smeltser, Executive VP & CFO, stated, “Net sales, which declined 10.2%… organic net sales decreased 11.1% primarily driven by targeted stop shipments to certain retailers, supply constraints and category softness.”

Outlook

  • Management reiterated its expectation to deliver approximately $160 million in free cash flow for fiscal 2025, noting, “we are reiterating our expectation to deliver the $160 million of free cash flow, and as I noted earlier, that is approaching $7 per share in free cash flow in fiscal '25” (Maura).
  • Spectrum Brands did not provide full-year earnings guidance due to “the continued unpredictable nature of global tariffs and global trade negotiations, particularly between the U.S. and China and some softening in the U.S. and Europe of consumer demand.”
  • The company signaled an improving Q4, with Maura stating, “we are already seeing the results with a very strong start to the fourth quarter from a big rebound in sales in July.”

Financial Results

  • Net sales in Q3 declined 10.2%, with organic sales down 11.1% after foreign currency adjustments.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $76.6 million, a decline of $17 million compared to last year when excluding investment income.
  • Gross margins contracted 110 basis points, attributed primarily to negative mix, tariffs, and inflation.
  • Adjusted diluted EPS increased to $1.24, with Smeltser attributing this to “reduced income tax expense, lower interest expense and the reduction in shares outstanding, partially offset by lower adjusted EBITDA.”
  • The company ended the quarter with a cash balance of $122 million and net debt of $559 million.
  • Share repurchases in Q3 totaled 900,000 shares, with approximately 4 million shares repurchased year-to-date for roughly $300 million.

Q&A

  • Madison Callinan, Unidentified Company: “Could you reasonably quantify how much sales you left on the table by stopping shipments and other internal actions in Q2? And what impact, if any, lingers into Q4?” CFO Smeltser: “we would probably estimate it's in the neighborhood of $30 million in Q3. And… it will be quite a bit less than that in Q4.”
  • Callinan asked about guidance reinstatement. CEO Maura responded, “it's still fluid, right?... it's just irresponsible to sit here and say, 'Oh, yes, we're going to predict this stuff like very accurately.' We are getting back… We're getting into a much more normalized operating rhythm.”
  • Will, CJS Securities, inquired about capital allocation and M&A strategy. Maura said, “the shares are dramatically undervalued. We keep buying them every single day… At the end of the day, I do want to do M&A. We chased the deal this quarter. Got really close, unfortunately got outbid.”
  • Carla Marie Casella Hodulik, JPMorgan, asked for color on the pet category and supply constraints. Smeltser explained, “if we didn't have stopped shipments and some product availability issues, we probably sequentially would have improved sales from Q2 to Q3… we've got our pricing in place and a new team in that business.”
  • Olivia Tong Cheang, Raymond James: “Of that $30 million, how much of that do you think you can make up or have made up already?” Smeltser: “probably half of it we've already recovered and goes to that solid July that David talked about… by the time we get through the full year, that will be an overall meaningful impact to our results.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concern over the quantification of lost sales and the lack of earnings guidance, with questions pressing for more clarity and quantification, indicating a slightly negative to neutral sentiment.
  • Management tone was defensive regarding the unpredictability of tariffs and deliberate in emphasizing the long-term health of the business. Maura stated, “we took our medicine and better days are already happening.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone shifted from cautious optimism and focus on supply chain transition to a more assertive stance on cost control, supply chain recovery, and early signs of sales rebound.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw a sharper decline in net sales (down 10.2%) compared to the previous quarter’s 6% decrease, and a greater focus on immediate cost reductions ($50 million in 90 days).
  • Management continued to withhold formal earnings guidance due to ongoing volatility, whereas in the previous quarter, they had already suspended guidance but emphasized the uncertainty as temporary.
  • Analysts’ questions shifted from sourcing strategy and tariff impact quantification to recovery of lost sales and M&A pipeline.
  • Management’s confidence increased regarding near-term recovery, with explicit references to improving July sales and expectations for Q4 rebound.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited persistent risks from volatile global tariffs and unpredictable trade negotiations, particularly between the U.S. and China.
  • Ongoing consumer softness in the U.S. and Europe was highlighted as a concern.
  • Temporary supply disruptions and halted shipments during pricing negotiations resulted in lost revenue, with some impacts expected to linger into Q4.
  • The strategic transaction for the Home & Personal Care business remains delayed, attributed to the current tariff landscape and geopolitical factors.

Final Takeaway

Spectrum Brands navigated an exceptionally turbulent Q3, marked by supply chain halts and aggressive cost reductions, in response to dramatic tariff increases. While net sales and gross margin suffered, decisive actions are credited with stabilizing the business, restoring supply, and setting the stage for a rebound in Q4. With $160 million in free cash flow targeted for the year and share repurchases ongoing, management emphasizes a strong balance sheet and strategic flexibility, positioning the company for recovery and growth as tariff headwinds abate and supply chains normalize.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Gladstone Commercial signals continued portfolio expansion with $79M in Q2 industrial acquisitions while maintaining 98.7% occupancy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Gladstone Commercial Corporation (GOOD) Q2 2025

Management View

  • David John Gladstone, CEO, highlighted steady performance in the industrial real estate sector, noting, "We remain confident headed into our next quarter beginning July 2025." He emphasized the acquisition of two industrial facilities for $78.95 million, bringing the portfolio's industrial concentration to 67% of annualized straight-line rents. Portfolio occupancy was maintained at 98.7% as of June 30, 2025. Gladstone stated that the company completed the sale of one office property for a gain of $377,000 and finished a sales-type lease on an industrial property, previously recognizing a selling profit of $3.9 million. The weighted average remaining lease term increased to 7.1 years, and the company deployed $79 million in capital for new acquisitions, achieving the "second consecutive quarter of increased acquisition volume." Gladstone outlined a continued focus on disciplined underwriting, high-quality assets, and strategic capital deployment.

  • Gary Gerson, CFO, reported, "FFO and core FFO per share available to common stockholders were $0.33 and $0.35 per share, respectively, for the quarter." Gerson also stated, "Our second quarter results reflected total operating revenues of $39.5 million with operating expenses of $25.1 million as compared to operating revenues of $37.1 million and operating expenses of $26.0 million for the same period in 2024." He discussed an increased net asset base to $1.2 billion, attributed to new acquisitions, and shared, "As of today, our remaining 2025 loan maturities are manageable at $3.1 million."

Outlook

  • Management indicated a continued focus on acquiring high-quality industrial assets that are mission-critical and accretive to the long-term strategy. The company plans to "selectively dispose of noncore assets to further improve the portfolio." There is active work to extend leases, capture mark-to-market opportunities, and support tenant growth through expansions, capital improvements, and build-to-suit projects. The team expects an uptick in acquisition volume after the summer and remains "well positioned to deploy capital into accretive industrial acquisitions."

Financial Results

  • FFO per share was $0.33, and core FFO per share was $0.35 for Q2 2025. Total operating revenues were $39.5 million against operating expenses of $25.1 million. Same-store rents rose by 6.4% for the six months ended June 30 compared to the prior year, driven by increased property expense recovery revenue and higher rental rates. Net assets increased to $1.2 billion due to acquisitions. The company sold 2.5 million shares under its ATM program, raising $38.1 million, and had $94.4 million of revolver borrowings outstanding as of June 30, 2025. Cash and availability under the line of credit stood at approximately $31 million at quarter end. The common stock dividend remained $0.30 per share per quarter.

Q&A

  • Gaurav Mehta, Alliance Global Partners: Questioned the acquisition pipeline and market activity. Arthur S. Cooper replied that six LOIs are out, with active review of about twenty transactions, and anticipates an uptick in deals post-summer.
  • Gaurav Mehta: Asked about the background of an industrial property sale. Cooper explained it was a Georgia property where the tenant exercised a purchase option.
  • Gaurav Mehta: Inquired about the incentive fee waiver. Cooper stated the decision is made quarterly, balancing alignment with shareholders and employee retention.
  • Craig Kucera, Lucid Capital Markets: Asked about G&A increase. Gerson confirmed it was due to core FFO adjustments and annual meeting expenses. Kucera further questioned leverage strategy, to which Gerson responded, "Rather not press leverage. I mean, if we had to a little bit, we would, but...our goal here is to try to get that leverage down again."
  • Kucera: Asked about lease renewal terms. Cooper provided that the recent renewal had a 2.5% uptick and the upcoming lease may see a 2% increase over a 10-plus year term. He expressed confidence in renewal prospects for 2026 and 2027 expirations.
  • Dave Storms, Stonegate: Inquired about cap rates and underwriting adjustments. Cooper stated cap rates could average "8.5% plus" and affirmed no changes to underwriting criteria, with "100% collections of our rent."
  • John Massocca, B. Riley: Asked about plans for revolving credit. Gerson outlined options including sales on the ATM, refinancing, and potential private placement. Massocca also questioned cap rates for office sales; Cooper noted recent sales had cap rates that worked for the company. Gerson clarified no material one-time items in rental revenue for Q2.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were detailed and persistent, probing on acquisition pipeline, leverage strategy, incentive fee waivers, cap rates, lease renewal spreads, and underwriting discipline. The overall tone was neutral, with focus on future activity and risk management.
  • Management maintained a confident and slightly defensive tone, repeatedly emphasizing disciplined underwriting and portfolio quality. Cooper used phrases like, "We are not seeing any meaningful impact at this point," and "We will not change our underwriting criteria and qualifications."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained confident, though with heightened emphasis on underwriting discipline and portfolio resilience. Analysts' sentiment remained inquisitive and focused on financial strategies and risk.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Acquisition volume increased from $73.25 million in Q1 (five properties) to $78.95 million in Q2 (two properties), marking back-to-back quarters of heightened activity. Portfolio industrial concentration rose from 65% to 67%. Occupancy improved slightly from 98.4% to 98.7%. FFO per share declined from $0.34 in Q1 to $0.33 in Q2. Net assets increased from $1.16 billion to $1.2 billion. Management continues to prioritize industrial asset acquisitions and has maintained its focus on disciplined underwriting. Leverage increased due to acquisitions, but management signaled a desire to reduce it going forward. Analysts’ questions shifted more toward funding strategies and lease renewal spreads this quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted continued macroeconomic uncertainty, including the impact of tariffs, financing conditions, and global supply chain dynamics. Arthur S. Cooper reiterated, "We certainly are focused on what impact, again, the macro may have, obviously, tariffs and otherwise, but we are not going to change our underwriting criteria." Analysts raised concerns about leverage, G&A expenses, cap rate trends, and the incentive fee waiver process. Management addressed these by emphasizing liquidity, discipline in acquisitions, and confidence in tenant quality and rent collections.

Final Takeaway

Gladstone Commercial Corporation reported another active quarter with increased industrial acquisitions, stable occupancy, and ongoing portfolio optimization. Management underscored its disciplined approach to underwriting and capital allocation, reinforced by strong rent collections, manageable debt maturities, and a growing industrial asset base. The company plans to maintain its acquisition momentum while managing leverage and selectively recycling capital from noncore assets to further strengthen its industrial portfolio.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Pagaya raises 2025 revenue guidance to up to $1.325B while expanding product suite and funding capacity
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Pagaya Technologies Ltd. (PGY) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Gal Krubiner announced "our second consecutive quarter of positive GAAP net income at a record high of $17 million" and highlighted that "total revenues of $326 million was also a record as well as our $126 million in revenues from fee less production costs." Krubiner stated, "Given the sustainability of our growth, unique economic attribution, improving corporate structure and increasing demand for our product, we are raising our full year financial outlook."
  • Krubiner detailed product and funding advancements, noting that "30% of originations come from point-of-sale and auto lending versus only 9% just a year ago" and referenced the "forward flow expansion with Castlelake," resulting in "roughly $5 billion in forward flow capacity since the end of 2024."
  • Krubiner emphasized the launch of new initiatives: "initiatives and products such as the Pagaya direct marketing engine, the affiliate optimizer and the FastPass solutions are a crucial part of our future growth."
  • President Sanjiv Das highlighted the scaling of prescreen marketing initiatives and affiliate partnerships, explaining, "We are helping to Prescreen existing customers using our models and send them offers through e-mail, direct mail and other channels."
  • CFO Evangelos Perros stated, "We reported record results across all our key metrics, each at or above the high end of our original second quarter guidance. Most importantly, our results underscore the consistency and profitability of our network volume growth."

Outlook

  • Perros announced, "For the third quarter of 2025, we expect network volume in the range of $2.75 billion to $2.95 billion, total revenue and other income in the range of $330 million to $350 million and adjusted EBITDA in the range of $90 million to $100 million. We expect GAAP net income in the range of $10 million to $20 million."
  • For the full year, management raised guidance, stating, "We are increasing our expected network volume range to $10.5 billion to $11.5 billion, total revenue and other income in the range of $1.25 billion to $1.325 billion and adjusted EBITDA in the range of $345 million to $370 million. We are increasing our GAAP net income for the year in the range of $55 million to $75 million."
  • Perros also noted, "Our third quarter and full year GAAP net income guidance includes the impact of several onetime items, including approximately $24 million in costs associated with the issuance of our corporate bond and costs associated with the early retirement of existing credit lines."

Financial Results

  • The company achieved a network volume of $2.6 billion, with personal loans as the largest contributor, up 23% year-over-year. Point-of-sale and auto volumes now comprise 30% of total volumes.
  • Revenue and other income was $326 million, a 30% increase from a year ago, with revenue from fees growing by 31%. Fee revenue less production costs (FRLPC) reached $126 million, representing 4.8% of network volume, and lending partner fees contributed 81% of total FRLPC.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $86 million, up 72% year-over-year, with a margin of 26.4%.
  • GAAP net income grew to $17 million, representing a 5% margin, and adjusted net income was $51 million.
  • The company issued $2.3 billion in ABS across six transactions and closed its first AAA-rated auto ABS and a $300 million AAA-rated point-of-sale ABS.
  • As of June 30, Pagaya held $242 million in cash and cash equivalents and $870 million of investments in loans and securities.

Q&A

  • Peter Corwin Christiansen, Citi: "Are you seeing some traction there potentially seeing some new partners on this side -- on the BNPL side coming in?" Krubiner: "Buy now, pay later is one of them, but we see that actually across the board in PL in buy now, pay later and in auto loan."
  • Christiansen: "It seems like this changes the glide path for capital structure planning as we think forward and certainly a reputational benefit here. Just wondering if you want to -- if there's anything you can add there on how you see this deal being transformational for Pagaya." Perros: "Maybe another way to think about it is that this bond effectively is a step function in our evolution and provides probably the biggest, call it, risk reduction in our business and our franchise as a whole."
  • Kyle Joseph, Stephens: "I'm just thinking how does this kind of impact your growth profile with the partner model, you had good growth, but it could be fairly lumpy. Is this something where you think about kind of smoothing out results?" Das: "We absolutely expect it to impact the growth in our PL business very soon. We expect to roll this out by the fourth quarter. So we expect 2026 is when we'll start to see the full impact of this."
  • John Douglas Hecht, Jefferies: "Do you guys see others entering? And what do you think the market direction is and the competitive dynamics are, especially kind of considering your kind of first-mover advantage?" Krubiner: "We are welcoming any type of ability of anyone to do and to go after that... the way we do it is very, very different."
  • Sanjay Harkishin Sakhrani, KBW: "Maybe just a little bit more detail around what you're seeing and what the path forward is in terms of credit quality?" Das: "We have seen across all our principal asset classes, but definitely in personal loans and auto that the consumer performance has been extremely good... we are being very cautious, but also very, I would say, tactically very smart in terms of making sure [indiscernible]."
  • Harold Lee Goetsch, B. Riley: "What are some of the workflows and assets you bring to do that?" Das: "What happens is because it's a bank, we first have to go through a very rigorous process of making sure that our models relative to the banks, both the risk management process as well as the bank's compliance process in terms of model approval... I just want to say thank you, everyone, for all the audience and all the investors supporting us throughout the years."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a positive tone, frequently congratulating management and focusing on expansion opportunities, capital structure, and product initiatives.
  • Management was confident throughout, emphasizing execution and strategic progress, with Krubiner stating, "the answer is yes" regarding new partnerships and Das projecting significant growth potential.
  • Compared to the prior quarter, both analysts and management displayed increased optimism, with more discussion of forward-looking growth, product rollout, and funding advancements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management raised full-year guidance for network volume, revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and GAAP net income versus the previous quarter.
  • The product focus advanced from prescreen proof-of-concept to the imminent rollout of direct marketing and affiliate engines, with expectations for a material impact in 2026.
  • Funding capacity increased with new forward flow agreements and a successful $500 million corporate bond issuance.
  • The previous quarter highlighted macro uncertainty and operating discipline, while the current call emphasized growth, product expansion, and scale.
  • Analysts in both quarters asked about scaling, profitability, and risk, but the current quarter featured more questions about product-driven growth and competitive differentiation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management referenced "the protracted uncertainty" in the macro environment and noted, "we remain cautious due to the protracted uncertainty."
  • The impact of one-time costs related to debt refinancing and tax matters was explicitly included in GAAP net income guidance.
  • Management reaffirmed a deliberate, conservative approach to credit and volumes to respond to any macro shifts.

Final Takeaway

Pagaya delivered record results in Q2 2025, underscored by strong profitability, expanded product offerings, and growing funding capacity. Management raised full-year guidance for all major metrics, driven by product-led growth initiatives and robust demand from current and prospective partners. The company highlighted its unique network model, disciplined credit approach, and operational leverage as foundations for ongoing profitable expansion, while remaining vigilant regarding macroeconomic risks and committed to prudent growth strategies.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Core Scientific holder to vote against CoreWeave deal unless terms improved
data.description:
A large Core Scientific (NASDAQ:CORZ) is threatening to vote against its planned sale to CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) unless terms of the deal are improved.

Two Seas Capital, which has a 6.3% stake in Core Scientific, plans to vote against the sale to CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) unless the terms of the transaction are changed.

"Unless the terms are revised to address what we believe to be both the structural shortcomings and inadequate consideration, we intend to vote against the transaction and plan to solicit you, our fellow Core Scientific shareholders, to do the same," Two Seas President and Chief Investment Officer Sina Toussi wrote in a letter to shareholders on Thursday. "We do not believe this transaction should receive shareholder approval."

The opposition comes after a Financial Times report earlier this week that some Core Scientific (NASDAQ:CORZ) holders are opposed to the proposed $9 billion deal, arguing it could leave them short-changed  and does not fairly reflect Core Scientific’s value.

Under the proposed agreement, Core Scientific shareholders would receive 0.1235 newly issued shares of CoreWeave for each share they hold. Many top shareholders and hedge funds are debating whether to reject the deal altogether, aiming to force improved terms or even to scuttle the transaction if their expectations are not met.

 

CoreWeave's (OTCQB:CRVW) stock has fallen by over 26% since the merger announcement, leading to a downward pressure on Core Scientific’s valuation from $20.25 per share to over $14 per share, leaving less for its shareholders.

Some investors believe Core Scientific is worth more than the original valuation, and should be bought for a figure closer to $25 a share, or $11.5bn, the FT reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Shares of CoreWeave (CRWV) rose 6.1% on Thursday, while Core Scientic (CORZ) ticked higher by 1.1%.

CoreWeave (CRWC) is scheduled to report Q2 results on Tuesday.

More on Core Scientific

data.title: Extreme Networks outlines $1.228B–$1.238B fiscal 2026 revenue target with accelerated SaaS ARR growth amid large enterprise wins
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Extreme Networks (EXTR) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Edward B. Meyercord highlighted that "Q4 marked our fifth consecutive quarter of sequential revenue growth. Revenue reached $307 million, representing a 20% increase year-over-year with particularly strong performances in APAC and EMEA regions." He underscored momentum in SaaS ARR, which accelerated to $208 million, up 24% year-over-year, and called attention to the company's record large deal activity, product bookings at an eight-quarter high, and competitive wins against larger industry players.
  • CEO Meyercord reported that Extreme achieved its largest APAC win ever with an "8-digit project win for the entire Japanese judiciary, including the Supreme Court," and expanded its leadership in high-density public venue deployments, noting recent wins such as MetLife Stadium for the 2026 World Cup Finals and major luxury resorts in the Middle East.
  • The launch of Extreme Platform 1, described as "the first and only networking vendor to offer a conversational multimodal, agentic AI-powered networking platform generally available to customers today," was emphasized as a critical differentiator, receiving positive feedback from early adopters.
  • CEO Meyercord stated, "We expect growth in fiscal '26 to accelerate. We have tremendous opportunities with large customers. Our competitive positioning has never been stronger, and we're accelerating investments in our business to drive automation, differentiation and commercial success."
  • CFO Kevin Rhodes said, "I'm very pleased to report strong fourth quarter results with revenue exceeding the high end of our guidance range. We also delivered strong operating margins and free cash flow. We achieved earnings per share of $0.25 at the high end of our guidance range and exceeding the consensus of $0.23, up 32% from $0.19 in the prior year quarter on an adjusted basis."

Outlook

  • CFO Rhodes provided guidance for fiscal 2026: "For the first quarter of fiscal 2026, we expect guidance as follows: revenue to be in a range of $292 million to $300 million; gross margin to be in the range of 61.9% to 52.3%, operating margin to be in a range of 12.7% to 14.5% and earnings per share to be in a range of $0.20 to $0.23."
  • For the full fiscal year 2026, Rhodes stated, "We expect revenue to be in a range of $1.228 billion to $1.238 billion."
  • Meyercord expressed confidence that "growth in fiscal '26 to accelerate," backed by a strong funnel and pipeline.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the company now signals a more pronounced acceleration in overall revenue and SaaS ARR growth, with a focus on large enterprise wins and new platform adoption.

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for the quarter was $307 million, up 20% year-over-year and 8% sequentially.
  • SaaS ARR reached $208 million, up 24% year-over-year, and product revenue was $192 million, a 26% increase year-over-year and 8% sequentially.
  • Free cash flow was $75 million, and EBITDA reached $50 million, both at multi-quarter highs.
  • Non-GAAP gross margin was 62.3%; operating margin was 15.2%.
  • Deferred SaaS revenue rose 15% year-over-year to $308 million; total deferred revenue surpassed $600 million.
  • Thirty-four customers spent over $1 million in the quarter, totaling 168 for fiscal 2025.

Q&A

  • Eric Martinuzzi, Lake Street Capital Markets: Asked about EMEA and APAC sustainability. Meyercord stated momentum is expected to continue, pointing to stabilized government environments and unique growth opportunities, with particular excitement around the Japanese government and partner ecosystem.
  • Martinuzzi followed up on tariff-driven Q4 order pull-forwards. Meyercord clarified, "very minimal, not like what we've seen -- not even close to what we may have seen in the past," due to product category exemptions.
  • Ryan Boyer Koontz, Needham & Company: Questioned Platform One's impact and MSP expansion. Meyercord responded that meaningful Platform One contributions will emerge in the second half of the year, noting growing trials and early adoption. On MSPs, Meyercord said, "we're still in early stages...we have a fully automated platform that is really simple to use," expecting momentum to build.
  • Koontz asked about upmarket opportunities post-HP Juniper. Meyercord highlighted an expanded funnel of large opportunities, referencing major recent wins and citing the company's fabric technology as a differentiator.
  • Christian Schwab, Craig-Hallum Capital: Queried if product bookings growth is product-driven or market share gains. Meyercord said, "it's more about the solutions and the features and the capabilities that we're bringing to market that are really differentiating us," and that competitor disruption is creating opportunities for Extreme.
  • Timothy Horan, Oppenheimer: Asked about pipeline impact from HP Juniper merger and MSP carrier prospects. Meyercord confirmed a "net positive" effect and outlined ongoing efforts to attract large telecom MSPs, emphasizing the company's modern multi-tenant MSP platform.
  • David Vogt, UBS: Inquired about gross margin sustainability. Rhodes stated, "We've been pretty consistent...at that 58% range...we could see that clipping up to 60%."
  • Vogt also sought demand trends by vertical. Meyercord responded that state, local, and education is about 40% of revenue, with other verticals like retail, manufacturing, and health care each about 10%.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed optimism about the quarter's results, but pressed for details on sustainability of regional momentum, Platform One adoption timing, and competitive positioning.
  • Management maintained a highly positive and confident tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently highlighting competitive wins and innovative platform features. Meyercord said, "Our competitive positioning has never been stronger."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone remained upbeat but focused more on long-term sustainability, while management's confidence increased, especially regarding new platform launches and large deal momentum.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to a more assertive stance on revenue acceleration and SaaS ARR growth, compared to the previous quarter’s cautious optimism.
  • Strategic focus has moved decisively to scaling Platform One, leveraging disruption at competitors, and deepening large enterprise penetration.
  • Analysts shifted from questions on tariff and supply chain risks to pressing for details on new platform adoption and upmarket competitive wins.
  • Key metrics such as SaaS ARR growth, free cash flow, and large customer wins accelerated.
  • Management’s confidence and forward-looking commentary advanced, indicating higher expectations for fiscal 2026.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged that while current product categories are tariff-exempt, "that could always change," indicating ongoing vigilance regarding trade policy.
  • Meyercord noted that meaningful adoption of Platform One will phase in over the second half of the year, implying some timing risk for ARR growth acceleration.
  • Meyercord referenced the need to "get in there and unlock and get one of these larger players" in the MSP space, signaling execution risk in this growth vector.
  • Competitive landscape changes—such as the HP Juniper merger and Cisco’s channel restructuring—present both disruption opportunities and the risk of intensified competition.

Final Takeaway

Extreme Networks delivered record revenue growth, robust SaaS ARR, and expanding large enterprise wins in Q4, propelled by differentiated solutions and the launch of Extreme Platform One. Management projects revenue acceleration into fiscal 2026, driven by strong pipeline visibility, strategic wins in key regions, and continued investment in automation and commercial models, while remaining vigilant on tariff risks and focused on scaling new platform adoption.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Sempra outlines $13B 2025 capital plan and affirms EPS guidance while advancing utility-focused strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sempra (SRE) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Jeffrey Walker Martin, Chairman, President & CEO, reported that "second quarter 2025 adjusted EPS of $0.89, which is in line with the prior period's results," and confirmed, "we're also affirming our full year 2025 adjusted EPS guidance range of $4.30 to $4.70, and we're also affirming our 2026 EPS guidance of $4.80 to $5.30."
  • Martin stated the company's capital plan targets "the investment of roughly $13 billion this year with over $10 billion allocated toward our growing U.S. utilities," and highlighted more than $5 billion of new capital deployed in the first half.
  • On capital recycling, Martin indicated Sempra has entered into "a nonbinding letter of intent with KKR" for an equity sale at Sempra Infrastructure, potentially "within or even above the 15% to 30% range depending upon valuation and other considerations."
  • Major progress was noted in the Ecogas sales process, with substantial interest received from strategic and financial parties. Both the Sempra Infrastructure and Ecogas transactions are anticipated to close mid-2026, with expectations for an accretive impact on EPS and credit.
  • Martin emphasized the transition "toward a more utility-focused business model," with increasing business mix weighted toward Texas through the end of the decade.
  • The Fit for 2025 campaign continues to drive productivity improvements, cost reductions, and organizational realignment, including technology adoption and streamlined processes.
  • On wildfire mitigation, Martin noted SDG&E has "hardened 100% of its transmission system with still structures in the highest fire threat areas or what we call Tier 3 zones."
  • Karen L. Sedgwick, Executive VP & CFO, stated "Oncor continues to present a truly compelling investment opportunity as the company continues to execute on its $36 billion 5-year capital plan."
  • Sedgwick highlighted the passage of Texas House Bill 5247, which established the Unified Tracker Mechanism (UTM) to reduce regulatory lag and improve earned ROE, with Oncor anticipating a 50 to 100 basis point increase in ROE over time.
  • SDG&E was awarded an estimated $600 million in transmission projects as part of the Cal ISO 2024 to 2025 transmission plan, with most investments occurring beyond the current capital plan period.
  • SDG&E filed a request with the CPUC targeting savings of approximately $300 million by phasing out regulatory programs, adding to $200 million of federal tax credits being passed to customers this year.
  • Sedgwick highlighted operational milestones at Sempra Infrastructure, including Cameron LNG Phase 1's 1,000th LNG cargo and progress at ECA LNG Phase 1 (94% complete) and Cimarron Wind (85% complete), with key revenue generation milestones expected in 2026.

Outlook

  • Management affirmed full year 2025 adjusted EPS guidance of $4.30 to $4.70 and 2026 EPS guidance of $4.80 to $5.30.
  • Both Sempra Infrastructure and Ecogas sales are projected to close mid-2026, with expected positive impacts on EPS and credit.
  • Incremental capital opportunities at Oncor for 2025-2029 remain under evaluation, with upward pressure on the $12 billion identified for potential expansion.
  • Port Arthur LNG Phase 2 is targeting FID in 2025, with all major permits now secured and a new 20-year SPA executed with JERA for 1.5 MTPA offtake capacity.

Financial Results

  • Sempra reported Q2 2025 GAAP earnings of $461 million or $0.71 per share and adjusted earnings of $583 million or $0.89 per share. This compares to Q2 2024 GAAP earnings of $713 million or $1.12 per share and adjusted earnings of $567 million or $0.89 per share.
  • Sempra California contributed $5 million primarily from higher regulatory awards, electric transmission margin, and AFUDC equity, partially offset by lower CPUC base operating margin and cost of capital, $37 million lower income tax benefits, and higher net interest expense.
  • Sempra Texas saw $6 million higher equity earnings due to invested capital and customer growth, partially offset by higher operating and interest expenses and lower consumption attributable to weather.
  • Sempra Infrastructure registered $26 million higher revenues from a contract modification and higher power volumes. The parent company recorded a $16 million increase primarily from higher income tax benefits and net investment gains, partially offset by higher net interest expense.

Q&A

  • Ross Allen Fowler, BofA Securities: Asked about the KKR LOI and potential equity sale range; Martin clarified the flexibility and priorities in optimizing equity value and balance sheet.
  • Fowler followed up on capital plan timing and confidence in executing additional capital in Oncor and Texas; Martin and Allen Nye, CEO of Oncor, provided detailed commentary on ongoing strong growth, transmission opportunities, and capital plan processes, noting "we're quite comfortable confirming that we expect them to be at the high end of that $12 billion or more."
  • Steven Isaac Fleishman, Wolfe Research: Inquired if the $12 billion is included in the current plan; Martin responded, "No, it does not include that. We're 80% of the $36 billion plan, and all of that represents upside to our base plan."
  • Fleishman asked about LOI timing with KKR and coordination with rising CapEx at Oncor; Martin stated, "We're not going to forecast the timing of it right now, but we're pleased with the progress we've made so far."
  • Fleishman questioned credit impact of potential deconsolidation; Martin explained thresholds for regulated utility earnings composition and flexibility in the transaction structure.
  • Nicholas Joseph Campanella, Barclays: Asked about ROFR extension expiration and California legislative developments; Martin described the extension as evergreen and emphasized engagement in legislative sessions focused on wildfire and affordability, with Caroline Winn adding operational detail on wildfire mitigation and affordability measures.
  • David Keith Arcaro, Morgan Stanley: Sought updates on LNG market and Port Arthur contracting; Justin Bird, CEO of Sempra Infrastructure, and Martin expressed a bullish outlook for US LNG and highlighted the project's positioning for demand in Europe and Asia.
  • Carly S. Davenport, Goldman Sachs: Asked about feasibility of reaching FID on Port Arthur Phase 2 in 2025; Martin and Bird confirmed continued progress and momentum. Davenport also queried timing of UTM filings and ROE improvement; Don J. Clevenger, CFO of Oncor, and Martin provided detail on anticipated cadence and impact.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts exhibited a constructive but probing tone, focusing on execution risk, transaction timing, and capital plan visibility, seeking clarity on Oncor's growth, equity sale timing, and legislative impacts.
  • Management maintained a confident and measured tone throughout, affirming guidance, emphasizing operational progress, and providing transparent updates on negotiations and legislative developments. Martin used phrases such as "we're pleased with the progress we've made so far" and "we feel very good about the backdrop."
  • Compared to Q1, management’s tone in prepared remarks remained confident, while Q&A responses were more detailed and addressed nuanced risks and timing, with less ambiguity on strategic initiatives. Analyst tone was similarly focused on execution risks and upside scenarios.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company reaffirmed its 2025 and 2026 EPS guidance, consistent with the previous quarter.
  • Management provided more detail on the capital recycling process, specifically the nonbinding LOI with KKR and the potential equity sale range, compared to Q1’s more general discussion.
  • Progress was made on legislative outcomes in Texas (HB5247), with specific regulatory updates and anticipated ROE improvements now quantified.
  • Q2 featured increased detail on project milestones at Sempra Infrastructure, including ECA LNG, Cimarron Wind, and Port Arthur LNG.
  • Analyst questions this quarter were more focused on transaction timing and capital allocation, with a continued emphasis on execution and legislative/regulatory developments.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing negotiations for Sempra Infrastructure and Ecogas sales, with limited disclosure until definitive agreements are reached.
  • Execution risk remains in timing and completion of the equity sale and matching proceeds with increasing CapEx needs.
  • Legislative and regulatory developments in Texas and California are pivotal, particularly for Oncor’s capital recovery, ROE, and customer affordability initiatives.
  • Management highlighted wildfire risk in California, with continued investments in mitigation and operational leadership.
  • Analysts probed for clarity on timing, regulatory approvals, and potential impacts on credit metrics and business mix.

Final Takeaway

Sempra’s second quarter 2025 call underscored steady execution on its value creation initiatives, with management reaffirming full-year EPS guidance and a $13 billion capital plan, while advancing toward a more utility-focused business model. Key strategic milestones include progress on planned equity sales, major legislative wins in Texas, and continued growth in regulated utilities, positioning the company for improved credit and earnings consistency. Management emphasized operational achievements, project delivery, and cost control as critical levers for value creation as it navigates ongoing transactions and regulatory developments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Market Voices: Crypto 401(k)s, Fed chair contenders, Fed rates
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, remarks and announcements that could impact markets, sectors or individual stocks. 

  • President Trump plans to sign an executive order on Thursday that will allow Americans to include alternative assets such as cryptocurrencies and private equity funds in their 401((k)) accounts. 

The executive order will direct the Labor Department to consult with federal agencies such as the SEC about what regulatory changes would be needed to allow for the inclusion of alternative assets in 401((k))s, according to Barron's. 

  • Federal Reserve Gov. Christopher Waller has emerged as a leading candidate to replace Jerome Powell as Fed chairman, according to Bloomberg.

Waller has already met with Trump's team about the job, which opens up in May 2026, but has not yet met with Trump personally. The two other top contenders are former Fed official Kevin Warsh and National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett, Bloomberg said, citing unnamed sources. 

“President Trump will continue to nominate the most competent and experienced individuals,” White House Spokesman Kush Desai said in a statement to Bloomberg. “Unless it comes from President Trump himself, however, any discussion about personnel decisions should be regarded as pure speculation.” 

  • Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta President Raphael Bostic said he sees the Fed implementing one rate cut before the end of the year, noting that the longer term impact of higher tariffs was unclear. 

“This question about whether tariffs are a one-time thing, or whether they’re going to be more persistent in their effects and might even cause structural changes, I think is perhaps the most important question that we have today,” Bostic said Thursday during a panel discussion, according to Bloomberg. 

“This has not been a one-time thing where you wake up on one day and everybody knows what all the tariffs are,” Bostic added.

data.title: Collegium raises 2025 revenue guidance to $760M as Jornay PM growth accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Collegium Pharmaceutical (COLL) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Vikram Karnani, President and CEO, reported, "We generated both top and bottom line growth in the quarter, including record revenue from Jornay and returned value to our shareholders through our share repurchases." Karnani highlighted strategic priorities: driving growth for Jornay PM, maximizing the pain portfolio's durability, and deploying capital to enhance shareholder value.
  • Karnani stated, "During our third full quarter of owning Jornay, prescriptions grew 23% year-over-year, and we generated record quarterly revenues of $32.6 million. We also generated another quarter of meaningful growth from our pain portfolio with a record $155.4 million in combined revenues, up 7% year-over-year."
  • Karnani announced, "Our Board recently approved a new $150 million share repurchase program authorized through December 2026."
  • Karnani also confirmed leadership updates: "Gino Santini, our Lead Independent Director, was appointed as Chairman of the Board. Additionally, Dr. Carlos Paya was elected by our shareholders to join the Board following his nomination last quarter."
  • Karnani raised 2025 financial guidance, stating, "We now expect to grow total revenue by approximately 19% year-over-year... We now expect Jornay revenue to be in the range of $140 million to $145 million, representing roughly 42% growth from 2024 pro forma revenue."
  • Scott Dreyer, Chief Commercial Officer, emphasized, "Jornay's market share of the long-acting branded methylphenidate market grew to 23%, up 7.6 percentage points year-over-year. Jornay has a broad and growing prescriber base, reaching an all-time high of over 26,000 prescribers in the second quarter, up 23%."
  • Dreyer noted, "In April, we completed the expansion of our sales force, adding approximately 55 new representatives, bringing the total ADHD sales force to approximately 180 representatives."
  • Colleen Tupper, CFO, stated, "We delivered strong financial results in the second quarter, growing revenue 29% and adjusted EBITDA 9% year-over-year, while making targeted investments to drive continued growth in Jornay PM."

Outlook

  • Colleen Tupper reported, "We now expect total product revenues in the range of $745 million to $760 million, a $10 million increase compared to our prior guidance range, resulting in a 19% growth year-over-year."
  • Tupper provided further detail: "We now expect Jornay revenue to be in the range of $140 million to $145 million in 2025 compared to our prior guidance of at least $135 million."
  • Adjusted EBITDA guidance was raised to a range of $440 million to $455 million, a $5 million increase from the prior range and a 12% increase year-over-year.
  • Adjusted operating expenses are expected in the range of $225 million to $235 million, a $5 million increase from the prior guidance range.
  • Management expects quarterly adjusted operating expenses to trend down in the second half of the year.

Financial Results

  • Tupper reported, "Net product revenues were $188 million, up 29% year-over-year. Jornay net revenue was $32.6 million. Belbuca net revenue was $52.6 million, up 1% year-over-year. Xtampza net revenue was $52.6 million, up 18% year-over-year. Nucynta Franchise net revenue was $46.4 million, up 4% year-over-year."
  • GAAP operating expenses were $73.3 million, up 69% year-over-year. Non-GAAP adjusted operating expenses were $61.9 million, up 104% year-over-year.
  • GAAP net income was $12 million compared to $19.6 million in Q2 2024.
  • Non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA was $105.1 million, up 9% year-over-year.
  • GAAP earnings per share was $0.38 basic, $0.34 diluted; non-GAAP adjusted EPS was $1.68.
  • The company generated $72.4 million in cash from operations and ended the quarter with $222.2 million in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities.

Q&A

  • Leszek Sulewski, Truist Securities: Asked about the target number of additional prescribers for Jornay PM, awareness levels, progress in the adult ADHD market, and whether the $300 million to $400 million peak sales estimate for Jornay PM will be revisited. Vikram Karnani responded that the peak sales guidance will be revisited after assessing expanded sales performance. Scott Dreyer explained, "What we're focused on is continued breadth of prescribing...26,000 this quarter, that's up 23% year-over-year. There's no sign of slowing down." Dreyer also highlighted, "Already Jornay has 20% of its business with adults...the growth by type is this. We grew 21% in [indiscernible]. We grew 33% in adult."
  • Sulewski followed up on investments in the pain portfolio. Dreyer noted, "We have about 100 salespeople...Every year as part of brand planning, we check in on targets and what we need from an investment standpoint, and that's consistent."
  • John Todaro, Needham & Company: Asked about specific levers for top-line growth in Q3 and developments in generic competition for the pain portfolio. Dreyer highlighted, "The expansion is going really great. We're seeing great early indicators...we saw 2,700 of those new targets wrote a prescription." Tupper provided, "There is no party that has the necessary combination of all the ingredients needed to launch...competitive generics against any product in our pain portfolio."
  • David A. Amsellem, Piper Sandler: Inquired about the types of assets targeted for M&A or BD beyond pain/CNS, and whether the company would consider noncommercial or late-stage assets. Karnani replied, "We remain very committed to being disciplined in our approach...to the extent that we can leverage our investments and our infrastructure that is already in place, that would be our ideal target from a BD perspective."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarification on Jornay's peak sales potential, prescriber growth, and business development strategy, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone as they sought confirmation of the sustainability of current trends rather than expressing skepticism or concern.
  • Management maintained a confident and positive tone throughout, frequently referencing strong commercial performance and financial flexibility. Quotes such as "We are well positioned for our next phase of growth, and I am confident in our ability to deliver on our financial and strategic commitments" (Karnani) and "We're pleased with the initial progress and expect to see additional benefits as we head into the second half of the year and 2026" (Dreyer) illustrate this.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts' tone remains generally confident, with management slightly more assertive due to upgraded guidance and analysts focused on confirmation of execution and long-term prospects.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company raised its 2025 revenue guidance to $745 million–$760 million from the previous $735 million–$750 million, and Jornay PM's guidance to $140 million–$145 million from at least $135 million previously, reflecting increasing confidence in growth drivers.
  • Adjusted EBITDA guidance was lifted to $440 million–$455 million from $435 million–$450 million.
  • Jornay PM prescriptions and prescriber base increased, with market share now at 23%, compared to 20.3% in Q1.
  • Management tone shifted from cautious optimism in Q1 to more confident assertions in Q2, underpinned by record revenues and expanded commercial investments.
  • Analysts continued to focus on key growth metrics for Jornay and generic risk for the pain portfolio, but with greater emphasis on the realization of recent investments.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified increased operating expenses, primarily due to investments in Jornay commercialization and sales force expansion, as a near-term cost pressure, but expects expenses to trend down in the second half.
  • On generic competition, Tupper stated, "There is no party that has the necessary combination of all the ingredients needed to launch...competitive generics against any product in our pain portfolio."
  • Analysts focused on the durability of the pain franchise and the path to expanded prescriber growth for Jornay, with management reiterating confidence in both areas.

Final Takeaway

Collegium Pharmaceutical delivered record revenue and raised its 2025 guidance, driven by accelerating growth in Jornay PM and consistent performance across its pain portfolio. The company expanded its sales force, launched new marketing campaigns, and authorized a $150 million share repurchase program, all while maintaining strong cash flow and a solid balance sheet. Management remains confident in the durability of its core franchises and committed to executing its strategy for growth and shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
